steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Define the variable relationship: $x$ is the price of a ticket sold during the event. The price before the event is 4 dollars more than during, so the before-event price is $x + 4$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Express total revenue: the team sells at least 150 tickets before the event at $(x+4)$ each, and at least 210 tickets during the event at $x$ each. Total revenue $= 150(x+4) + 210x$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Apply the minimum requirement: the team needs to raise at least 5,970 dollars, so the inequality is $150(x+4) + 210x \ge 5{,}970$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $150(x+4) + 210x \le 5{,}970$ — uses the correct revenue expression but the wrong direction; "at least" requires $\ge$, not $\le$. Eliminated.
  • B: $150(x+4) + 210x \ge 5{,}970$ — correct revenue expression and correct direction. B is correct.
  • C: $150(x-4) + 210x \le 5{,}970$ — subtracts 4 from $x$ for the before-event ticket price, but the before-event price should be $x+4$ (higher, not lower). Also uses wrong direction. Eliminated.
  • D: $150(x-4) + 210x \ge 5{,}970$ — correct direction but wrong price relationship; subtracting 4 from before-event price is backwards. Eliminated.
